verifyProof(bytes32,bytes).details: In production, this would verify a Groth16/PLONK proof:      1. Deserialize proof points (A, B, C for Groth16)      2. Compute public input hash from commitment      3. Perform pairing check: e(A, B) == e(alpha, beta) * e(vk_x, gamma) * e(C, delta)

returns.valid: Whether the proof would be valid

verifyProof(bytes32,bytes).notice: Verify a ZK proof for a KYC commitment

verifyProofView(bytes32,bytes).notice: View function to check proof validity without state changes
